name: Hushkin
concept: A permanently-asleep bear cub who has settled into a soft loaf, clutching the long droopy tail of its own knitted nightcap like a comfort blanket — the pom-pom resting in its lap as the only thing it will never let go of.
imagePrompt:
A plush toy bear cub sitting upright and facing the viewer, fast asleep. Full body, centred, symmetrical, sculpted as a single connected soft-toy form with no separate pieces.

SILHOUETTE: a fat rounded loaf — a wide, low, bread-loaf trapezoid body with gently domed top and softly rounded corners, wider at the base than the shoulders, so it reads as a small heavy pillow that happens to have a face. The head is fused directly into the body with no neck, a big circle occupying the upper half. Two small semicircular ears sit low on the sides of the head, one of them squashed under the hat. Two stubby rounded arms rest forward on the belly; two tiny rounded feet, toes up, poke out at the bottom front like two little buns. Chunky, thick, no thin parts anywhere, no whiskers, no loose fluff, no stray fibres — a clean, definite outer edge like a die-cut felt shape.

THE ONE IDEA: a soft knitted nightcap, far too long, flops sideways off the head and its long tapering tail curves DOWN and ACROSS the bear's body — hugged tight against the belly by both stubby arms — ending in a big fat pom-pom sitting in the bear's lap. The bear is cuddling its own hat like a security blanket. The hat tail must remain thick (a plump knitted tube) and touch the body along its whole length, so hat, arms and body form one unbroken shape.

MATERIALS: unmistakably plush. The body is short-pile cream fleece with visible soft nap and a faint centre seam running down the belly; the muzzle and belly patch are smooth felt appliqué with a slightly raised edge and neat visible blanket-stitch around them; the nightcap and its pom-pom are chunky ribbed knitted wool with legible fat purl stitches and a cable-knit brim. Small honest toy-making details: a seam over the crown of the head, a stitched line where each arm joins.

PALETTE: warm oatmeal-cream fleece body (the light, comforting base), one bold hero colour — deep blueberry indigo — for the whole nightcap, brim and pom-pom; soft dusty-rose felt blush ovals on the cheeks; a pale butter-cream muzzle and belly patch; dark cocoa-brown for the stitched features. Warm, cosy, low-contrast lighting; soft rounded shading like a photograph of a well-loved toy on a shelf.

FACE: enormous warmth, zero cynicism. The eyes are two thick, generously curved embroidery-thread arcs curving downward in a contented sleeping smile-shape — bold, chunky stitches, never thin lines. A big rounded triangular cocoa nose, stitched, sitting on the pale muzzle, with a tiny stitched smile beneath curving up at the corners. Cheeks flushed. The whole face should say: this creature is having the best dream of its life and would like you to have one too.

PROPORTIONS: head roughly 45% of total height, body wide and heavy, limbs tiny — top-heavy and adorable, the weight sitting low so it looks like it could never tip over. Nothing finer than a thick stitch anywhere. No text, no symbols, no floating elements.
